VEDANTA'S ZAMBIA COPPER ARM GETS GOING-CONCERN WARNING : MC REPORT

• 🇿🇲 Vedanta-owned CopperTech Metals disclosed a going-concern warning in its SEC filing ahead of planned NYSE listing

• 📉 Auditors flagged substantial doubt over the ability of Konkola Copper Mines (KCM) to continue as a going concern

• 💰 KCM reported operating loss of $302.4 million for FY25

• 💸 Operating cash outflow stood at $265.9 million during FY25

• 🏗️ Vedanta is seeking funds for a $2.7 billion expansion of its Zambian copper operations, including deep mine development and processing upgrades

• 📊 CopperTech has applied for NYSE listing under ticker "CUX"

• ⚖️ KCM was under provisional liquidation following a dispute with the Zambian government before Vedanta regained control

🟠 Impact: Neutral to Slightly Negative (Disclosure highlights financial stress at KCM, but the warning relates to historical financials and comes alongside a major expansion and fundraising plan aimed at turning around operations.)

Gold Digital Trading to Go Live on NSE

NSE is set to fully launch its Electronic Gold Receipts (EGR) segment in the next ten weeks.

Now you’ll be able to buy and sell gold just like shares.

Key Highlights-:

-Only guaranteed 995 and 999 (24-carat) purity.

-Facility to purchase from 1 kilo down to just 100 milligrams.

-Timing: Trading from 9:00 AM to 11:30 PM.
-Gold will be securely stored in SEBI-regulated lockers.

3% GST will apply on physical delivery,
but 0% GST for trading on the exchange.

However, retail volumes may take a bit of time to ramp up as major brokers are enabling it gradually.
Are you ready to trade in digital gold?

text:
DEE DEVELOPMENT : ORDER BOOK JUMPS TO RECORD ₹2,434 CR 🚀📈

• 📦 Total order book rose to ₹2,433.9 Cr as of May 31, 2026, versus ₹1,909.8 Cr at the start of the month 

• 🆕 Fresh order inflow during May stood at ₹631.9 Cr, while execution was ₹107.8 Cr 

• ⚡ Power segment order inflow remained strong at ₹278 Cr+, taking power order backlog to ₹1,436 Cr+ across India and Thailand operations 

• 🛢️ Oil & Gas order inflow of ₹352 Cr+ helped backlog rise to ₹882 Cr in DEE India business 

• 📊 FY27 cumulative order inflow reached ₹681.9 Cr, while cumulative execution stood at ₹177.5 Cr till May-end 

• 🔋 Malwa Power FY27 projected revenue estimated at ~₹47.7 Cr, including ₹23.4 Cr from pellet plant operations 

• ⚖️ Punjab & Haryana High Court stay continues, allowing DEE Power Division to supply electricity at ₹7.47/unit, with tariff dispute pending final resolution 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strong monthly order inflow, record order book of ₹2,434 Cr and healthy visibility across Power & Oil & Gas segments support future growth.)

text:
SIGMA ADVANCED SYSTEMS WINS ₹208 CR EXPORT DEFENCE ORDER 🎯💣

• 🇺🇸 Secures export contract worth US$21.97 Mn (~₹208 Cr) from a North American customer for 40,000 units of 155mm M107 artillery shell bodies 

• ⏳ Order to be executed over 6 months, with production and deliveries completed within the period 

• 🚀 Marks Sigma's expansion from a fuze manufacturer to a complete artillery shell component manufacturer 

• 🏭 Manufacturing requires advanced metallurgy, forging, machining, heat treatment and quality assurance capabilities 

• 🌍 155mm calibre remains the global standard across NATO and allied artillery platforms, supporting strong demand outlook 

• ✅ Export authorization already obtained from the Department of Defence Production (DDP), Ministry of Defence 

• 📈 Strengthens Sigma's position in global defence supply chains and creates opportunities for future ammunition manufacturing programs 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Large export order, entry into artillery shell manufacturing, enhanced defence capabilities and stronger global defence market positioning.)

text:
Bernstein On Asset Managers 

HDFC AMC and NAM fund performance trends have been incredibly strong over the past two years, with both seeing flow market shares rise. 

 The same is also true for ICICI Pru AMC (albeit listed later).  

FY23-26 saw performance trends and brand both favor the larger players.  

Brand is relevant for retail flows. 

If performance trends do not inch back up soon, the ‘performance + brand’ combo could become a ‘performance v. brand’ dynamic. 

low market share gains could be tested, at a time when equity returns and flow momentum are not as rosy as they once were.
channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

text:
CLSA on IT 

Sector alive, may not be kicking for everyone but definitely for aggressive managements 

Frontier model companies like Anthropic and OpenAI recently launched services arms as part of their broader partnership programme with system integrators (SIs) and consulting partners. 

This signifies a shift towards achieving deeper integration within enterprises as the intelligence layer gets largely commoditised.  

There exists a significant opportunity for system integrators to help enterprises realise the full potential from AI.  

Retooling of existing workflows, context engineering, model routing and building SLMs will increasingly require SI assistance in a multi-agent and multi-cloud reality 

Persistent Systems and Coforge, with limited legacy managed service businesses as clear beneficiaries.  

Among large caps, Tech Mahindra, Cognizant and Accenture will likely gain market share, at least in FY27.
channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

HDFC SECURITIES ON STYLAM INDUSTRIES

Upgraded to BUY from ADD; target price raised to ₹3,775 (30x March 2028 estimated EPS)

Governance concerns resolved following the exit of one promoter group, leading to a valuation multiple restoration to 30x from 20x

Partnership with Japan-based Aica Kogyo is seen as a key strategic catalyst, though synergy benefits remain excluded pending full 40% stake acquisition

FY27 laminates revenue growth target of 20-25% YoY, supported by upcoming plant commissioning; acrylic revenues expected to scale significantly from a low base

Brownfield laminate expansion (3.3 billion capex) deferred to mid-July 2026 due to environmental clearance; the plant holds ~10 billion revenue potential

Domestic business expected to recover from Q1FY27, with the full impact of corrective measures anticipated by October 2026

Chemical cost pressures from Middle East tensions partially offset by price hikes, though full pass-through remains challenging compared to European peers

text:
MORGAN STANLEY ON WIPRO

Maintains UNDERWEIGHT rating; target price remains at ₹192

Near-term growth outlook remains clouded by portfolio and client-specific challenges, though no incremental deterioration was observed in Q1 FY27

Projects Wipro to continue lagging behind industry peers in quarter-on-quarter revenue growth

Maintains medium-term margin guidance of 17-17.5%, but warns that upcoming wage hikes may suppress margins below this range in the near term

Notes that revenue compression stems from severe industry-wide competition rather than just Al-driven productivity gains being passed to clients

Observes that modern deal structures are blurring the lines between organic and inorganic growth, with strategic contracts often requiring upfront productivity commitments

Highlights a non-linear Al adoption curve where client spend involves the simultaneous cannibalization of existing services and the buildup of new Al-focused offerings

Jefferies report interesting read. Basically saying one thing clearly: AI data center demand is not the problem. Physical supply is the bottleneck. The world wants far more data center capacity than the ecosystem can build and commission fast enough. The cleanest theme: AI infrastructure bottlenecks and their beneficiaries. The best names are the ones sitting at real choke points where demand is exploding but supply cannot scale easily. Key buckets: Cooling:
[$VRT](https://x.com/search?q=%24VRT&src=cashtag_click)
,
[$CARR](https://x.com/search?q=%24CARR&src=cashtag_click)
,
[$TT](https://x.com/search?q=%24TT&src=cashtag_click)
,
[$JCI](https://x.com/search?q=%24JCI&src=cashtag_click)

,

,

,

The big takeaway: The next layer is physical infrastructure: power, cooling, land, construction, grid, and capacity. That is where the real bottleneck sits.

ALLIED BLENDERS & DISTILLERS FACES ₹25.54 CR CLAIM ⚖️

• 📄 Former MAIIPL director Balaji Shivdas Pawar has filed a Statement of Claim before a sole arbitrator

• 💰 Claim amount stands at ₹25.54 Cr, along with 18% annual interest from the date of filing until realization

• 📝 Claim relates to alleged outstanding dues under a Deed of Retirement-cum-Admission dated December 10, 2024

• 🏢 Claim has been filed against MALIIPL (subsidiary of ABDL), Allied Blenders & Distillers and another party

• ⚖️ Company has appointed legal counsel and plans to file a detailed defense and counterclaim

• 🛡️ ABDL stated the claims are partly/fully unsustainable, based on factual misstatements and contrary to records

• 📌 Company believes it has a robust defense and is taking necessary steps to safeguard its interests

🟠 Impact: Neutral to Slightly Negative (Litigation-related development; financial exposure is limited relative to company size, but the dispute may create near-term overhang until resolution.)

text:
RELIANCE INFRASTRUCTURE SEEKS REVIEW OF ASM RESTRICTIONS 📢

• 🏛️ Reliance Infrastructure has formally approached SEBI, NSE and BSE seeking a review of the IBC-linked surveillance framework and ASM restrictions on its shares

• 👥 Company said the current framework is adversely impacting over 7 lakh retail shareholders

• 📉 Under the existing rules, the stock trades only once a week with a ±5% price band, leading to predictable and mechanical price movements

• 💰 Reliance Infra argued that shareholders are often unable to exit investments at reasonable prices during lower circuits, resulting in erosion of value

• 🔄 Company proposed a calibrated approach retaining safeguards such as 100% margin, ASD and price-band controls, while enabling better price discovery

• ⚖️ Reliance Infra highlighted that NCLAT has stayed both the insolvency order and CIRP, and no Resolution Professional has assumed control of the company

• 📋 Requested alternatives include a periodic call-auction mechanism or a wider graded price band to facilitate genuine two-sided trading

🟢 Impact: Positive (If regulators review or relax restrictions, it could improve liquidity, price discovery and investor sentiment for Reliance Infrastructure shareholders.)

text:
GOVT MANDATES STANDARD PACK SIZES FOR EDIBLE OILS 🛢️📦

• 🇮🇳 Government directs edible oil makers and importers to sell oils only in fixed standard pack sizes

• 📏 Packaging restricted to 9 standard sizes ranging from 200 ml to 20 litres

• 🏷️ Volume-based packs must also display equivalent weight to improve price comparison

• 🛒 Move aims to help consumers easily compare prices across brands and identify best-value products

• 🌻 Rules cover both domestic and imported edible oils including palm, soybean, sunflower, mustard and groundnut oils

• 🔹 Packs below 200 ml and certain minor edible oils exempted to maintain affordability

🟢 Impact: Neutral to Slightly Positive (Improves transparency and consumer convenience; limited operational adjustments required for edible oil companies.)

• 📈 India's wholesale inflation touched a 42-month high of 8.3% in April 2026, up from 3.9% in March

• ⛽ Fuel & power inflation surged to 24.7%, while mineral oil inflation jumped to 39.5%

• 🌍 West Asia conflict and rising crude prices are driving energy-led inflation globally

• 🏭 Higher input costs impacting manufacturing, chemicals, transport and infrastructure sectors

• 🏗️ Despite near-term volatility, long-term themes remain intact: infrastructure, manufacturing, electrification and renewable energy

• 🔩 Zinc demand supported by galvanised steel and infrastructure buildout

• 🔌 Copper demand driven by electrification and power network expansion

• ☀️ Silver demand backed by solar energy and electronics manufacturing

• 🔋 Critical minerals gaining importance in energy storage and advanced manufacturing supply chains

• 🇮🇳 Resource security and domestic industrial capacity are becoming key priorities globally

🟢 Impact: Positive for Vedanta & critical mineral players (Supports long-term demand outlook for zinc, copper, silver and strategic minerals despite short-term commodity volatility.)

text:
INDIA DATA CENTRE CAPACITY MAY REACH 5–8 GW BY 2030: BERNSTEIN 🏢⚡

• 📈 India's data centre capacity could surge from 1.5 GW currently to 5–8 GW by 2030

• 🌏 Growth driven by AI demand and capacity constraints in other Asian markets

• 🏗️ Key competitive advantage will be access to land, power substations and grid connectivity, not compute hardware

• 📍 Navi Mumbai already witnessing intense competition for land, with prices rising above previous transaction levels

• 💰 Data centre infrastructure capex in India estimated at around ₹50 Cr per MW (excluding compute hardware)

• 🤝 Bernstein expects Indian players to focus on the colocation model, leasing infrastructure to hyperscalers who bring their own GPUs

• ⚡ Players with large land banks, secured power connectivity and thermal equipment access are expected to dominate

Likely Beneficiaries 🇮🇳

• 🏢 Anant Raj • 🏢 Black Box • 🏢 Techno Electric • 🏢 Adani Enterprises (AdaniConneX) • 🏢 Bharti Airtel (Nxtra) • 🏢 Reliance Industries • ⚡ Power Grid Corporation • ⚡ NTPC • 🔌 KEC International, Kalpataru Projects, Hitachi Energy India

🟢 Impact: Positive (Reinforces the long-term investment cycle in data centres, power infrastructure, transmission and digital infrastructure.)

text:
RBI's Hidden Liquidity Play

ECB and FCCB flows plunged 30% in FY26 to $42.9B. The RBI is keeping repo rates steady at 5.25% while managing supply shocks, forcing a structural pivot toward alternative dollar mobilization.

The consensus treats this status quo as a pause, missing the liquidity trap. By absorbing hedging costs for FCNR-B deposits, the central bank is aggressively subsidizing domestic deposit growth

This regulatory alpha triggers a regime shift. Swapping external debt for domestic credit transmission protects corporate margins from overseas de-rating, even as CPI inflation peaks at 5.9%.

Bottom Line: Stop watching the repo rate headline. The real alpha is in the backend forex reforms designed to cap domestic yields and guarantee credit growth without bleeding the currency.

text:
Paint industry commentary from Indigo Paints gives a very interesting insight into the current demand trend

Management highlighted that the paint industry had gone through a fairly long weak cycle, with almost 8 consecutive quarters of muted demand. After nearly 2 years of softness, demand started improving meaningfully from Nov’25 onwards.

But just when demand was recovering, the Middle east conflict war created fresh supply chain disruption. Mar'26 saw a sharp spike in raw material costs & tight availability of critical inputs.

The most important insight is around volume growth versus value growth.

Indigo management pointed out that most paint companies have been reporting volume growth significantly higher than value growth for the last 7-8 quarters.

This clearly suggests that economy & budget products are growing much faster than premium products for most paint companies.

Management said that in their case, value growth has generally been higher than volume growth across categories.

Management also said that they have been gradually premiumising their product portfolio over the last 2-3 years.

Overall paint sector is seeing demand recovery after a weak phase.

text:
Sector wise weightage of Global Markets - India remains the most diversified market in MSCI Index

India Has the Lowest Sector Concentration Among Major Markets with top 3 sector constitute only 52% of MSCI India Index. 

Other Global markets:
Taiwan: 96%
Korea: 86%
Brazil: 68%
China: 64%
Japan: 60%
USA: 58%

India's market is spread across Financials, IT, Industrials, Consumer, Healthcare, Materials, Energy and other sectors.

Over the past 1 year, AI trade has benefitted Taiwan & Korea, not India. A large part is because global capital chased Semiconductors, AI Infrastructure and Hardware Supply Chains. 

Returns:
Korea: 120%
Taiwan: 92%
EM: 42%
India: -13%

Foreign investors have favored AI-linked markets where technology weights are extremely high.

Taiwan IT weight: 88%
Korea IT weight: 61%
USA IT weight: 35%
India IT weight: 8%

Long-term performance still remains strong though India's growth has come down in past 1 year from May'21 to May'26 in USD terms

May'21 - May'25 - Growth: 8%
May'21 - May'26 - Growth: 4%

Any reversal or moderation in AI led trade could significantly benefit the diversified markets such as India.

IDFC FIRST BANK: KPMG FORENSIC REVIEW CONFIRMS CHANDIGARH FRAUD 🚨🏦

• 🔍 KPMG forensic review confirmed fraudulent activities at the Sector 32, Chandigarh branch

• 👥 Fraud involved collusion between certain employees/ex-employees, employees of government departments and third parties

• 📍 Incident was isolated to a single branch; no similar cases found across other branches

• 💰 Net principal amount involved remains around ₹646 Cr, in line with earlier disclosures

• ✅ Bank has already repaid the concerned government departments along with applicable interest and accounted for it in Q4 FY26

• 🖥️ Core Banking System (CBS) records were found to be accurate, with customer balances and transactions properly reflected

• 📧 Customers received regular account statements and transaction alerts as per process

• 🛡️ Bank has implemented enhanced controls including centralized oversight, stronger customer communication and technology-led monitoring systems

• 🚔 Bank stated it is a victim of the fraud and continues to cooperate with investigative authorities

🟡 Impact: Neutral (No increase in financial impact versus earlier disclosures, incident remains isolated and control framework has been strengthened, though governance concerns persist.)

• 🌱 HCLTech announced winners of the 3rd Climate Action Grant in the Americas, with total funding of $1 million

• 🇬🇹 Guatemala-based CISP won the top grant of $500,000

• 🇦🇷 Aves Argentinas and 🇲🇽 Lluvia para Todos received $250,000 each

• 💧 Funds will support water security, biodiversity conservation, ecological restoration and rainwater harvesting projects

• 🌍 Program received applications from 10 countries, marking a 41% increase since launch

• 🌳 Previous grant-supported initiatives have enabled planting of 360,000+ native trees and mangroves

• 💰 HCLTech has committed $5 million over five years for the Climate Action Grant initiative

🟡 Impact: Neutral (CSR and sustainability initiative enhances brand value and ESG credentials but has no material impact on HCLTech's financial performance.)

KV TOYS TO ACQUIRE 50% STAKE IN PLAY PANDA 🧩🎲

• 🤝 KV Toys board approves acquisition of 50% equity stake in Play Panda Private Limited

• 💰 Acquisition to be completed for up to ₹4.5 Crore cash consideration, payable in tranches

• 🎯 KV Toys will gain joint management control in the target company

• 📚 Play Panda operates in educational toys, STEM learning aids, cognitive learning games and board games

• 🧲 Product portfolio includes magnetic puzzles, educational kits, STEM development puzzles, art & craft products and toddler learning toys

• 📈 Acquisition aimed at expanding KV Toys' market share, product range and presence in the fast-growing educational toys segment

• ⏳ Transaction expected to be completed within 3 months, subject to due diligence and definitive agreements

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens KV Toys' presence in the high-growth educational and STEM toys market while expanding its product portfolio and distribution opportunities.)

• 📍 Mine area spans 981.75 hectares

text:
ANDHRA CEMENTS TO MERGE WITH SAGAR CEMENTS 🏭

• 🔄 Board approves amalgamation of Andhra Cements (ACL) with parent Sagar Cements (SCL)

• 📊 ACL is a subsidiary of Sagar Cements, with SCL holding 75% stake

• 🤝 Merger aims to simplify group structure, eliminate duplication and improve operational efficiency

• ⚙️ Expected benefits include better resource utilisation, supply chain optimisation, cost synergies and stronger governance

• 📈 Combined entity to gain from larger scale, wider distribution reach and enhanced market presence

• 📝 Share swap ratio approved: 29 equity shares of Sagar Cements (FV ₹2) for every 98 equity shares of Andhra Cements (FV ₹10)

• ⏳ Scheme subject to approvals from shareholders, creditors, NCLT, SEBI, NSE, BSE and other regulators

🟢 Impact: Positive (Corporate restructuring simplifies group structure, unlocks synergies and strengthens operational efficiency for Sagar Cements.)
channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

text:
"There are certain years in company's journey where performance improves, and there are certain years where foundation for next decade is built. 

For Shilpa Medicare, FY26 has been one such year."

— Keshav Bhutada, CEO

Q4 FY26 , Concall .
END

Final crux:

Shilpa's transformation is underway.

What was once an oncology API company is now evolving into a multi-platform specialty pharma business, with investments made over the last decade beginning to enter the monetization phase.

Mgmt :
"We see a clear path towards sustainable growth, stronger margins and increased return ratios in coming years."

Guidance: 👉constructive on FY27

While avoiding numerical guidance, mgmt's commentary was positive.

Expectations for FY27:
✅ Steady API growth.
✅ Good formulation growth.
✅ Healthy Europe growth.
✅ Continued CDMO momentum.
✅ Similar level of licensing income.

Margin expansion remains an aspiration.

🗣"35% is an ambitious target, which we would be working towards achieving."

Mgmt : 
"We don't project any meaningful contribution coming from OLC in FY27." 

Mgmt: 👉
"We have been growing since past 2, 3 years at a very healthy rate, and we expect that trajectory to continue." 🔥

Biologics and ADCs are entering the monetization phase🔥

Biologics revenues doubled to ₹150 Cr in FY26.

Progress across the platform:
✅ Aflibercept launch targeted in FY27.
✅ Nivolumab entered clinical stage.
✅ Four new biosimilars added.
✅ Two NBE programs entering Phase I studies in FY27.

Shilpa also completed development of its first ADC biosimilar using in-house:

• Payload
• Linker
• Conjugation

The company is now building a fully integrated ADC manufacturing platform.

"With that integrated manufacturing capability, it will be one of its kind in India"💡

NorUDCA is shaping up into a major franchise

Shilpa became the first company globally to obtain approval for NorUDCA in NAFLD.

The company has partnered with 3 leading players in India and also launched its own brand, Noduca™.

Global expansion has begun.

Mgmt highlighted strong clinical outcomes and increasing traction.

"The product is really doing good, and we see very good clinical results in the patient."

More clarity on long-term trajectory is expected after completion of the six-month treatment cycle.

Formulations are emerging as the biggest growth engine

FY26 formulation revenues reached ₹618 Cr (+30%).

Excluding licensing income:
🔹 Q4 growth: 64%
🔹 FY26 growth: 75%

European formulation revenues crossed ₹200 Cr.

Upcoming launches include:
✅ Rotigotine Patch (Europe) – FY27
✅ Ondansetron ER Injection – FY27
✅ Enzalutamide filing – FY27
✅ Abraxane launch – FY28

Mgmt expects healthy growth to continue.

"There is likely to be a good growth possibility in FY27 in the formulation division." 🔥

APIs remain the backbone while CDMO is  becoming meaningful

API revenues grew 16% to ₹985 Cr.

Key developments:
🔹 15+ new oncology APIs added.
🔹 New oncology block under expansion.
🔹 OLC block commissioned.
🔹 Semaglutide validation completed.
🔹 Peptide capacity expansion underway.
🔹 New global clients added.

Importantly, CDMO has already become a sizeable business:💡

🔹 API CDMO revenue ~₹110 Cr
🔹 Biologics CDMO revenue is of similar scale

"Overall, API business is likely to have a steady growth in FY27."🔥

FY26:
🔹 Revenue: ₹1,549 Cr (+18%)
🔹 EBITDA: ₹445 Cr (+30%)
🔹 EBITDA Margin: 29%
🔹 Adj. PAT: ₹232 Cr (+135%)

Adjusted ROCE (excluding biologics/NBE investments) improved sharply from 4% in FY23 to 17.4% in FY26

Mgmt :
"We are on track for a stronger FY27."

Shilpa Medicare Q4 FY26: Building the foundation for the next decade 👉

Shilpa is no longer a single vertical.
It is building a pharma platform spanning:

✅ APIs
✅ Complex Formulations
✅ CDMO
✅ Biologics
✅ ADCs
✅ Recombinant Human Albumin

Finance Min says 

-Govt opens Portfolio Investment Scheme (PIS) to all individual Persons Resident Outside India (PROIs).

-Individual PROI investment limit in listed companies raised to 10% from 5%.

-Overall investment limit for all individual PROIs increased to 24% from 10%.

-Govt expands Fully Accessible Route (FAR) to include 15-year, 30-year and 40-year G-Secs.

-Sovereign Green Bonds included under Fully Accessible Route.

-Govt removes short-term investment limit for FPIs in government securities.

-Govt removes concentration limit for FPIs in government securities.

-Govt removes security-wise limit for FPIs investing in government securities.

-Overall FPI investment cap retained at 6% for Central G-Secs and 2% for State G-Secs.

-Govt merges 'general' and 'long-term' FPI investment limits for government securities.

-Govt exempts FPIs from tax on interest income from government securities.

-Govt exempts FPIs from tax on capital gains from government securities.

-Tax exemption for FPIs on G-Secs effective from April 1, 2026.

-Similar tax exemption on G-Secs extended to Bank for International Settlements (BIS)

CITI ON HDFC ASSET MANAGEMENT

Maintains NEUTRAL rating; target price ₹2,800

HDFC Mutual Fund has imposed temporary restrictions on lump sum subscriptions for HDFC Gold ETF and HDFC Gold ETF Fund of Funds (FoF).

Direct lump sum subscriptions of ₹25 crore or more in HDFC Gold ETF are restricted effective June 8, 2026

Lump sum and switch-in transactions for the HDFC Gold ETF FOF are capped at 10 lakh per PAN per month, effective June 5, 2026

Citi characterizes the impact on HDFC AMC's overall revenues as negligible, estimating the contribution from incremental gold ETF flows at less than 1%

Urban premium consumption is the hide; while 

Tractors, Entry 2W, Rural FMCG, and MFIs are the pain trade.

Citi On India electrical Equipment

Power grids are emerging as the key bottleneck to energy transition. 

BNEF expects sing renewable penetration, data centers and industrial growth to drive ~US$15tn in global T&D capex over CY25-50. 

We see India as uniquely positioned (it manufactures ~80% of the global T&D products) to benefit 
from a large domestic transmission buildout, accelerating HVDC adoption, favourable localization policies, and growing export opportunities. 

Initiate Buy on Hitachi Energy/POWERIND (TP Rs46,700), GVTD (TP Rs6,200) and CGPI (TP R$1,100), and Neutral on Siemens Energy/ENRIN (TP Rs4,000).

**How BioPharm Strengthens Indegene**

**Acquisition Overview**
- Deal Value Up To $106M
- Largest Revenue Acquisition Yet
- Serves 17 Top Pharma Clients
- Expands Commercialization Capabilities

**What BioPharm Brings**

**HCP Media & AdTech**
- Programmatic HCP Engagement Platform
- Omnichannel Media Capabilities

- Direct Brand-Level Relationships

**Tandem Data Platform**
- 3M+ HCP Profiles

- Built Over Two Decades

**Data Advantage**
- Physician Profiles Expand Sharply
- Strong Oncology Coverage Added

- Ophthalmology Coverage Strengthened

**Completing The Commercial Funnel**

**Upstream Layer**
- CultHealth Drives Creative Strategy
- Tectonic Supports Brand Development

**Execution Layer**
- ECS Manages Content Production
- Campaign Execution Capabilities

**Data & Media Layer**
- BioPharm Enables Media Activation
- Addressable HCP Targeting Added

- Measurement And Optimization Tools

- No Structural Capability Gaps

- Proprietary Data Difficult To Replicate

- Creates Long-Term Differentiation

**Integration Progress**
- Support Transition Underway
- Omnichannel Wins Already Visible

- FY26 Integration Remains Key

**Key Takeaway**
- BioPharm Adds Revenue And Capability
- Tandem Platform Is Core Asset
- Completes Data And Media Layer
- Strengthens Indegene's Pharma Ecosystem

Why do companies like HFCL and Sterlite Tech run up like crazy? Read the AI fibre intensity part below.

The binding constraint to watch: not demand — it is power availability, grid connectivity, and cooling retrofit capacity. Whoever removes those bottlenecks captures disproportionate economics. This is precisely why the equipment layer, not the operator layer, is where the alpha sits.

Government approved 100 billion in budgetary support to Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs) for Aviation Turbine Fuel (ATF) price stabilization

Support facilitates interest-free advances to OMCs to help them absorb losses when global ATF prices exceed set benchmarks

Mechanism aims to provide price predictability and shield airlines from extreme fuel cost volatility caused by the West Asia crisis

Fund operates on a true-up model: OMCs will repay the government when global fuel prices moderate

Analyst scenario analysis indicates the support could cover 2-5 months of fuel volatility, depending on price points

No specific estimate changes were made pending further clarity on the fuel price cap mechanism
